100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 30, 1926, American aviator Charles Lindbergh completed a historic transatlantic flight, setting new records and captivating the nation with his daring spirit. In Europe, the League of Nations faced ongoing challenges as tensions rose in various regions, highlighting the fragile peace following the Great War. Meanwhile, in the United States, the Senate debated an ambitious farm bill aimed at addressing the economic struggles of rural communities.
